children.name: Fulmarus glacialis
children.description: A common seabird of the North Atlantic and North Pacific. Grey above, white below, with a thick, tubenosed bill. Breeds on sea cliffs around the British Isles, Iceland, and Norway.

children.name: Fulmarus glacialoides
children.description: The Antarctic counterpart of the Northern Fulmar. Pale grey above, white below, with a bluish-grey bill. Breeds in large colonies on Antarctic coasts and islands.

children.name: Macronectes giganteus
children.description: A huge, aggressive petrel of the Southern Ocean. Dark brown or white morphs exist. Breeds on subantarctic islands.

children.name: Macronectes halli
children.description: Slightly smaller than Southern Giant Petrel, with a paler bill tip. Breeds on subantarctic islands.

children.name: Pagodroma nivea
children.description: A stunning pure white petrel that breeds deep in the Antarctic continent. Its ghostly white plumage provides camouflage against the snow.

children.name: Thalassoica antarctica
children.description: A striking brown-and-white petrel that breeds in huge colonies on the Antarctic continent.

children.name: Daption capense
children.description: A distinctive black-and-white petrel with a checkered wing pattern. Boldly follows ships.

children.name: Aphrodroma brevirostris
children.description: A dark grey petrel with a short, stout bill, breeding on the Kerguelen, Crozet, and Prince Edward Islands.

children.name: Puffinus puffinus
children.description: A classic shearwater of the North Atlantic. Black above, white below, with stiff wingbeats close to the water.

children.name: Puffinus mauretanicus
children.description: A critically endangered shearwater endemic to the Balearic Islands. Brownish-grey above, dirty white below.

children.name: Puffinus yelkouan
children.description: A medium-sized shearwater of the Mediterranean, breeding on islands and coastal cliffs. Classified as Vulnerable.

children.name: Puffinus huttoni
children.description: Breeding only in two high-altitude colonies in New Zealand. Classified as Endangered.

children.name: Puffinus lherminieri
children.description: A small shearwater found throughout tropical and subtropical Atlantic and Caribbean waters.

children.name: Puffinus bailloni
children.description: A small shearwater of tropical Indian and Pacific Oceans, formerly considered a subspecies of Audubon's.

children.name: Puffinus baroli
children.description: The smallest shearwater in the North Atlantic, breeding on the Azores, Canaries, and Madeira.

children.name: Puffinus nativitatis
children.description: An all-dark brown shearwater found in the central Pacific, named after Christmas Island.

children.name: Puffinus subalaris
children.description: A small shearwater endemic to the Galapagos Islands.

children.name: Puffinus gavia
children.description: A small shearwater of New Zealand and southern Australia, named for its rapid, fluttering flight.

children.name: Puffinus opisthomelas
children.description: A medium-sized shearwater of the Pacific coast of Baja California, classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Puffinus auricularis
children.description: A Critically Endangered shearwater breeding only on Socorro Island off Mexico.

children.name: Puffinus newelli
children.description: A shearwater endemic to the Hawaiian Islands, classified as Endangered due to predation and light pollution.

children.name: Puffinus myrtae
children.description: A small shearwater endemic to the island of Rapa in French Polynesia, classified as Critically Endangered.

children.name: Puffinus assimilis
children.description: A small black-and-white shearwater breeding on islands of the south Atlantic and around Australia and New Zealand.

children.name: Puffinus elegans
children.description: A small shearwater breeding on the Antipodes Islands and Tristan da Cunha.

children.name: Puffinus bannermani
children.description: A small shearwater endemic to the Bonin Islands of Japan, classified as Endangered.

children.name: Puffinus heinrothi
children.description: A small shearwater breeding in the Bismarck Archipelago, poorly known.

children.name: Puffinus boydi
children.description: A small shearwater endemic to the Cape Verde Islands, sometimes considered a subspecies of Barolo.

children.name: Calonectris diomedea
children.description: A large shearwater of the Mediterranean. Brownish-grey above, white below, with a yellowish bill. Breeds on Mediterranean islands.

children.name: Calonectris borealis
children.description: A large shearwater of the North Atlantic, breeding on the Azores, Madeira, and the Berlengas.

children.name: Calonectris edwardsii
children.description: A large shearwater endemic to the Cape Verde Islands, classified as Near Threatened.

children.name: Calonectris leucomelas
children.description: A large shearwater breeding on islands of Japan and Korea, wintering south to Australia.

children.name: Ardenna gravis
children.description: Breeding on Tristan da Cunha and Gough Island, undertaking a figure-of-eight migration across the Atlantic.

children.name: Ardenna creatopus
children.description: Breeding on islands off Chile, with distinctive pink legs and feet.

children.name: Ardenna bulleri
children.description: Breeding only on the Poor Knights Islands of New Zealand, with a distinctive dark M across the wings.

children.name: Ardenna pacifica
children.description: A large shearwater with a wedge-shaped tail, common in tropical Pacific and Indian Oceans.

children.name: Ardenna tenuirostris
children.description: Breeding in huge colonies in Tasmania and Bass Strait, with a circular migration across the Pacific.

children.name: Ardenna carneipes
children.description: A large all-dark shearwater with pale pink legs, breeding around Australia and New Zealand.

children.name: Ardenna grisea
children.description: A large all-dark shearwater that undertakes one of the longest migrations of any bird, breeding in the Southern Hemisphere and migrating to the North Pacific and North Atlantic.

children.name: Pterodroma feae
children.description: Breeding on Cape Verde and Madeira. Grey above, white below with a dark M across the wings.

children.name: Pterodroma hasitata
children.description: Breeding in the mountains of Hispaniola. Distinctive black cap, white collar, and white rump. Endangered.

children.name: Pterodroma heraldica
children.description: A variable gadfly petrel of tropical Pacific and Indian Oceans.

children.name: Pterodroma solandri
children.description: Breeding on Lord Howe Island, it was named Providence Petrel for providing food for early settlers.

children.name: Pterodroma leucoptera
children.description: A small gadfly petrel with a white collar, breeding on Cabbage Tree Island off eastern Australia.

children.name: Pterodroma brevipes
children.description: A small gadfly petrel of the tropical Pacific, breeding on remote forested islands.

children.name: Pterodroma cervicalis
children.description: A large gadfly petrel with a white collar, breeding on the Kermadec Islands.

children.name: Pterodroma externa
children.description: Breeding only on Alejandro Selkirk Island off Chile.

children.name: Pterodroma phaeopygia
children.description: Breeding only in the highlands of the Galapagos Islands. Critically Endangered.

children.name: Pterodroma sandwichensis
children.description: Breeding in the Hawaiian Islands on high volcanic slopes. Vulnerable.

children.name: Pterodroma cahow
children.description: Thought extinct for 300 years until rediscovery in 1951. Breeds only in Bermuda.

children.name: Pterodroma madeira
children.description: One of Europe's rarest seabirds, breeding only in the mountains of Madeira. Endangered.

children.name: Pterodroma deserta
children.description: Breeding only on Bugio Island in the Desertas Islands off Madeira.

children.name: Pterodroma incerta
children.description: Breeding on Gough Island and Tristan da Cunha. Endangered due to mouse predation.

children.name: Pterodroma mollis
children.description: Breeding on subantarctic islands across the Southern Ocean.

children.name: Pterodroma macroptera
children.description: A large all-dark petrel breeding on subantarctic islands.

children.name: Pterodroma neglecta
children.description: A highly variable petrel occurring in dark, intermediate, and light morphs, breeding across the tropical Pacific.

children.name: Pterodroma atrata
children.description: A dark gadfly petrel endemic to Henderson Island in the Pitcairn Islands.

children.name: Pterodroma inexpectata
children.description: Breeding in the mountains of South Island, New Zealand, migrating across the Pacific.

children.name: Pterodroma ultima
children.description: A large gadfly petrel breeding on remote islands of the tropical Pacific.

children.name: Pterodroma gouldi
children.description: Breeding on islands around northern New Zealand, formerly considered conspecific with Great-winged Petrel.

children.name: Pterodroma magentae
children.description: One of the world's rarest seabirds, breeding in the Chatham Islands. Critically Endangered.

children.name: Pterodroma cookii
children.description: A small gadfly petrel breeding on islands off New Zealand, migrating to the North Pacific.

children.name: Pterodroma defilippiana
children.description: A small gadfly petrel breeding in the Juan Fernandez Archipelago off Chile.

children.name: Pterodroma longirostris
children.description: Breeding on Alejandro Selkirk Island off Chile, migrating to the northwest Pacific.

children.name: Pterodroma pycrofti
children.description: A small gadfly petrel breeding on islands off New Zealand.

children.name: Pterodroma alba
children.description: A medium-sized gadfly petrel of the tropical Pacific, breeding on the Phoenix and Line Islands.

children.name: Pterodroma arminjoniana
children.description: A variable petrel breeding on Trindade Island off Brazil.

children.name: Pterodroma lessonii
children.description: A large gadfly petrel with a distinctive white head, breeding on subantarctic islands.

children.name: Pterodroma nigripennis
children.description: A widespread gadfly petrel of the tropical Pacific, breeding on islands from the Kermadecs to French Polynesia.

children.name: Pterodroma axillaris
children.description: Breeding only in the Chatham Islands of New Zealand. Endangered.

children.name: Pterodroma hypoleuca
children.description: A small gadfly petrel breeding on islands of Japan and the Hawaiian Islands.

children.name: Pterodroma baraui
children.description: Breeding only on Reunion Island in the Indian Ocean. Endangered.

children.name: Pterodroma occulta
children.description: A recently described gadfly petrel breeding on Vanikoro and the Banks Islands of Vanuatu.

children.name: Procellaria cinerea
children.description: A large grey-brown seabird with white underparts. Near Threatened.

children.name: Procellaria aequinoctialis
children.description: A large all-dark petrel with a white chin patch. Vulnerable due to longline bycatch.

children.name: Procellaria conspicillata
children.description: Breeding only on Inaccessible Island in Tristan da Cunha. Vulnerable.

children.name: Procellaria parkinsoni
children.description: Breeding only on Great Barrier and Little Barrier Islands off New Zealand.

children.name: Procellaria westlandica
children.description: Breeding only on the West Coast of New Zealand's South Island. Endangered.

children.name: Bulweria bulwerii
children.description: A small all-dark petrel breeding on islands across the tropical Atlantic and Pacific.

children.name: Bulweria fallax
children.description: A medium-sized dark petrel of the northwest Indian Ocean.

children.name: Pseudobulweria rostrata
children.description: A large dark petrel of the tropical Pacific, breeding on islands from Fiji to the Marquesas.

children.name: Pseudobulweria becki
children.description: A poorly known petrel rediscovered in the Solomon Islands in 2007.

children.name: Pseudobulweria macgillivrayi
children.description: One of the world's least-known seabirds, known from only a single specimen from Gau Island, Fiji.

children.name: Pseudobulweria aterrima
children.description: A critically endangered petrel breeding on Reunion Island in the Indian Ocean.

children.name: Pachyptila desolata
children.description: The most abundant prion species, with an estimated 50 million individuals.

children.name: Pachyptila belcheri
children.description: A small prion with a distinctive slender bill.

children.name: Pachyptila turtur
children.description: The smallest prion species.

children.name: Pachyptila crassirostris
children.description: A prion with a stouter bill than other prions.

children.name: Pachyptila vittata
children.description: The largest prion, with a distinctive broad, flattened bill.

children.name: Pachyptila salvini
children.description: A medium-sized prion very similar to the Antarctic Prion but with a slightly narrower bill.

children.name: Halobaena caerulea
children.description: A beautiful small petrel with blue-grey upperparts, distinguished from prions by its black cap and white-tipped tail.

children.name: Pelecanoides urinatrix
children.description: A small auk-like petrel that dives underwater for crustaceans, breeding across the Southern Ocean.

children.name: Pelecanoides georgicus
children.description: A small diving petrel breeding on South Georgia and subantarctic islands.

children.name: Pelecanoides garnotii
children.description: A diving petrel of the Humboldt Current off Peru and Chile. Endangered.

children.name: Pelecanoides magellani
children.description: A diving petrel found in the cold waters of southern South America.
